metadata.title: Implement SLA Calculator and Tracking Integration

user_story.i_want: SLA tracking integrated into the ticket lifecycle with priority-based deadline calculation
user_story.so_that: incidents have deadline enforcement and agents can prioritize work based on SLA risk in the Zero-Hunt Agent Console

context.summary: Epic 2 requires SLA countdown display in the Priority Queue (Story 2.10-2.13). SLA calculation must be implemented before frontend work begins. This story creates the SlaCalculator service, SlaTracking domain entity, and integrates SLA tracking into the TicketService operations from Story 2.2.
context.scope: Only INCIDENT tickets get SLA tracking. Service requests follow approval workflows without time-based SLAs.

ac.title: Create SlaTracking entity
ac.description: Create `SlaTracking` entity at `backend/src/main/java/io/monosense/synergyflow/itsm/internal/domain/SlaTracking.java` with UUIDv7 primary key, fields (id, ticketId, priority, dueAt, breached, createdAt, updatedAt, version), unique constraint on ticketId, foreign key to tickets table

ac.title: Create SlaTrackingRepository
ac.description: Create `SlaTrackingRepository` at `backend/src/main/java/io/monosense/synergyflow/itsm/internal/repository/SlaTrackingRepository.java` extending JpaRepository with query methods: findByTicketId, findByDueAtBeforeAndBreachedFalse

ac.title: Create Flyway migration for sla_tracking table
ac.description: Create Flyway migration `V11__create_sla_tracking_table.sql` (CORRECTED from V8 - V8/V9/V10 already exist) with table structure, indexes on ticket_id and due_at, foreign key constraint, unique constraint on ticket_id

ac.title: Create SlaCalculator service
ac.description: Create `SlaCalculator` service at `backend/src/main/java/io/monosense/synergyflow/itsm/internal/service/SlaCalculator.java` with method calculateDueAt(Priority, Instant) returning startTime + duration based on priority (CRITICAL: 2h, HIGH: 4h, MEDIUM: 8h, LOW: 24h)

ac.title: Integrate SLA tracking into TicketService.createTicket()
ac.description: After ticket persistence in createTicket(), if ticket type is INCIDENT and priority is not null, call slaCalculator.calculateDueAt(), create SlaTracking entity, save via slaTrackingRepository

ac.title: Integrate SLA recalculation into TicketService.updatePriority()
ac.description: After priority update in updatePriority(), if ticket type is INCIDENT, fetch existing SLA record, recalculate dueAt from original ticket.createdAt, update and save SLA record

ac.title: Skip SLA updates for resolved/closed tickets
ac.description: updatePriority() must check ticket status before recalculating SLA. If status is RESOLVED or CLOSED, skip SLA recalculation to preserve historical accuracy

ac.title: Optimistic locking for concurrent SLA updates
ac.description: SlaTracking entity has @Version field, concurrent priority changes trigger OptimisticLockException, TicketService @Retryable handles retries

ac.title: Create SlaCalculatorIntegrationTest
ac.description: 6 integration test scenarios: IT-SLA-1 (SLA created on incident creation), IT-SLA-2 (SLA skipped for service request), IT-SLA-3 (SLA skipped without priority), IT-SLA-4 (SLA recalculated on priority change), IT-SLA-5 (SLA frozen for resolved ticket), IT-SLA-6 (SLA created when priority added later)

ac.title: Create SlaTrackingConcurrencyTest
ac.description: 2 concurrency test scenarios: IT-SLA-7 (concurrent priority changes with retry), IT-SLA-8 (unique constraint enforcement)

ac.title: Create SlaTrackingLoadTest
ac.description: 2 load test scenarios: LT-SLA-1 (1000 incidents/minute with SLA tracking, p95 <400ms), LT-SLA-2 (500 priority updates/minute with recalculation, p95 <400ms, retry exhaustion <10%)

ac.title: Create SlaCalculatorTest
ac.description: 6 unit test scenarios: UT-SLA-1 to UT-SLA-4 (each priority level calculation), UT-SLA-5 (null priority exception), UT-SLA-6 (timezone independence)

ac.title: Create SlaTrackingServiceTest
ac.description: Unit tests mocking repository and calculator dependencies, testing SLA creation and recalculation logic, verifying edge cases (null checks, status checks)

subtasks.subtask: Create Flyway migration V11__create_sla_tracking_table.sql (CORRECTED)
Define table structure with columns, indexes, constraints
Test migration with ./gradlew flywayMigrate in local environment
Verify indexes created with EXPLAIN queries
Create SlaTracking.java entity in internal/domain/ package
Add JPA annotations, UUIDv7 generator, optimistic locking
Add getters/setters and @PrePersist/@PreUpdate lifecycle methods
Create unit test for entity instantiation and field validation
Create SlaTrackingRepository interface in internal/repository/ package
Define query methods: findByTicketId, findByDueAtBeforeAndBreachedFalse
Add repository integration test (Testcontainers) verifying CRUD operations
Create SlaCalculator service in internal/service/ package
Implement calculateDueAt(Priority, Instant) method with duration mapping
Add Javadoc with examples and edge case notes
Create SlaCalculatorTest with 6 unit tests (UT-SLA-1 to UT-SLA-6)
Inject SlaCalculator and SlaTrackingRepository into TicketService
Modify createTicket() to create SLA record for incidents with priority
Modify updatePriority() to recalculate SLA (skip if resolved/closed)
Add null checks and type checks (INCIDENT only)
Update existing TicketService unit tests to mock SLA components
Create SlaCalculatorIntegrationTest with Testcontainers PostgreSQL
Implement IT-SLA-1 to IT-SLA-6 (creation, skipping, recalculation, frozen SLA)
Create SlaTrackingConcurrencyTest with IT-SLA-7 and IT-SLA-8
Verify SLA records in database after each test scenario
Test edge cases: null priority, service requests, resolved tickets
Create SlaTrackingLoadTest with @Tag("load")
Implement LT-SLA-1 (1000 concurrent creations with SLA tracking)
Implement LT-SLA-2 (500 concurrent priority updates with recalculation)
Run load tests with ./gradlew test -DincludeTags=load
Document p95/p99 latencies and retry exhaustion rates
Create ADR: "ADR-011: SLA Tracking with Priority-Based Deadlines"
Update docs/architecture/data-model.md with sla_tracking table schema
Update Epic 2 Tech Spec completion status (Story 2.3 → Done)
Code review: verify all ACs met, test coverage >85%
Demo: Create incident, show SLA record in database, update priority, verify recalculation

doc.title: Epic 2 ITSM Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Story 2.3: Implement SlaCalculator (8 points) - Create SlaCalculator.java with calculateDueAt (priority-based: Critical 2h, High 4h, Medium 8h, Low 24h), Create SLA tracking record on ticket creation, Update SLA due date on priority change, Add unit tests
doc.reason: Authoritative source for Story 2.3 scope, acceptance criteria, and SLA policy

doc.title: Epic 2 ITSM Technical Specification
doc.reason: Shows exact integration pattern for SLA tracking in TicketService.createTicket()

doc.title: Solution Architecture
doc.snippet: - OLTP schema for tickets/issues/approvals + audit/outbox (Flyway V1). - Read models: ticket_card, queue_row, issue_card, board_view, sprint_summary. - Primary keys: UUIDv7 (Hibernate 7 @UuidGenerator(style = VERSION_7) or custom fallback); time-ordered for index locality.
doc.reason: Defines UUIDv7 primary key standard and database schema approach

doc.title: Module Boundaries
doc.snippet: - internal/domain/: Domain entities (package-private) - internal/repository/: JPA repositories (package-private) - internal/service/: Business logic services (package-private) - spi/: Cross-module interfaces (@NamedInterface) - api/: REST controllers (public)
doc.reason: Defines package structure and visibility rules for ITSM components

doc.title: UUIDv7 Implementation Guide
doc.reason: Shows exact annotation pattern for UUIDv7 primary keys in entities

doc.title: Story 2.2 TicketService Implementation
doc.section: Approved - TicketService with state machine and retry pattern
doc.snippet: TicketService implements 10+ state transition methods, @Retryable optimistic lock handling, event publishing to outbox, TicketQueryService SPI
doc.reason: Foundation for Story 2.3 - provides TicketService to integrate SLA tracking into

doc.title: Story 2.1 Ticket Domain Entities
doc.snippet: Created Ticket.java (base entity), Incident.java (extends Ticket), ServiceRequest.java (extends Ticket) with UUIDv7 PKs, JPA repositories, Flyway migrations
doc.reason: Provides domain model foundation - Ticket and Incident entities referenced by SlaTracking

artifact.reason: Target for SLA integration - needs SlaCalculator and SlaTrackingRepository injection, modify createTicket() and updatePriority() methods
Enum values (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) determine SLA duration in calculateDueAt() method
Base entity for incidents - provides ticketType, priority, createdAt fields used in SLA calculation logic
Subtype of Ticket - only incidents get SLA tracking (not service requests)
Enum containing INCIDENT and SERVICE_REQUEST - used to filter which tickets get SLA tracking
Contains RESOLVED and CLOSED status values - used to skip SLA updates for resolved/closed tickets (AC-7)
Example JPA repository pattern - SlaTrackingRepository will follow same structure
Latest migration is V10 - Story 2.3 should create V11__create_sla_tracking_table.sql (NOT V8 as originally stated)

dependency.name: spring-boot-starter-data-jpa
dependency.usage: JPA entity and repository support for SlaTracking

dependency.name: spring-retry
dependency.usage: TicketService @Retryable handles OptimisticLockException for SLA updates

dependency.name: testcontainers-postgresql
dependency.usage: Integration tests with realistic PostgreSQL environment

interface.name: SlaCalculator.calculateDueAt
interface.usage: Called by TicketService.createTicket() and updatePriority() to calculate SLA deadline based on priority

interface.name: SlaTrackingRepository.findByTicketId
interface.usage: Called by TicketService.updatePriority() to fetch existing SLA record for recalculation

interface.name: TicketService.createTicket
interface.usage: MODIFY this method to call slaCalculator.calculateDueAt() and save SLA record after ticket persistence

interface.name: TicketService.updatePriority
interface.signature: void updatePriority(UUID ticketId, Priority newPriority, UUID currentUserId)
interface.usage: MODIFY this method to recalculate SLA when priority changes (skip if resolved/closed)

constraint.name: Package-private visibility
constraint.description: All ITSM internal components (domain, repository, service) must be package-private. Only api/ and spi/ packages are public.
constraint.enforcement: Spring Modulith ModularityTests verifies internal/ not accessible from other modules

constraint.name: UUIDv7 primary keys
constraint.description: All entities must use UUIDv7 (@UuidGenerator style VERSION_7) for time-ordered primary keys and better index locality
constraint.enforcement: Story 2.1 established pattern, docs/uuidv7-implementation-guide.md provides implementation guide

constraint.name: Optimistic locking with @Version
constraint.description: Entities with concurrent updates must have @Version field for optimistic locking. TicketService @Retryable handles OptimisticLockException.
constraint.enforcement: Story 2.2 established retry pattern with exponential backoff (50ms, 100ms, 200ms with jitter)

constraint.name: Flyway migration numbering
constraint.description: Latest migration is V10. Story 2.3 must create V11__create_sla_tracking_table.sql (NOT V8 as originally stated in story - V8/V9/V10 already exist)
constraint.enforcement: Flyway version numbering enforced by migration tool

constraint.name: Service Layer Pattern
constraint.description: SlaCalculator is a stateless service with pure calculation logic (no database access). TicketService handles persistence.
constraint.enforcement: Separation of concerns improves testability - calculator can be unit tested without database

constraint.name: Integration tests with Testcontainers
constraint.description: All persistence-layer tests must use Testcontainers PostgreSQL for realistic environment. No embedded/H2 databases.
constraint.enforcement: Story 2.1/2.2 established pattern - see SlaCalculatorIntegrationTest, SlaTrackingConcurrencyTest

constraint.name: Load tests with @Tag("load")
constraint.description: Performance-critical features must have load tests with @Tag("load") validating p95/p99 latencies under concurrent load
constraint.enforcement: Run with ./gradlew test -DincludeTags=load, document results in story completion notes

constraint.name: Unit test coverage >90% for services
constraint.description: SlaCalculator is simple logic - achieve >90% coverage with 6 unit tests (UT-SLA-1 to UT-SLA-6)
constraint.enforcement: Gradle test coverage report, code review validation

constraint.name: CRUD operations p95 <400ms
constraint.description: SLA tracking must not degrade ticket creation/update performance. Target p95 <400ms (same as Story 2.2 baseline without SLA)
constraint.enforcement: LT-SLA-1 and LT-SLA-2 load tests validate performance targets

constraint.name: Index on ticket_id for O(1) lookup
constraint.description: sla_tracking table must have index on ticket_id column for fast lookups by TicketService.updatePriority()
constraint.enforcement: CREATE INDEX idx_sla_tracking_ticket_id ON sla_tracking(ticket_id) in V11 migration

standards.integration_tests: Testcontainers PostgreSQL 16 for realistic database environment

test.name: IT-PERSISTENCE: SLA entity persistence and repository operations
test.description: Verify SlaTracking entity can be persisted, queried by ticket_id, and cascade deleted with ticket. Test unique constraint on ticket_id.

test.name: UT-CALCULATOR: Priority-based duration mapping
test.description: Test calculateDueAt() for all Priority values (CRITICAL=2h, HIGH=4h, MEDIUM=8h, LOW=24h), null priority exception, timezone independence

test.name: IT-CREATE: SLA created on incident creation
test.description: Create CRITICAL incident via TicketService.createTicket() → verify sla_tracking record exists with due_at = created_at + 2h. Verify no SLA for service requests.

test.name: IT-UPDATE: SLA recalculated on priority change, frozen for resolved tickets
test.description: Create LOW incident → change priority to CRITICAL → verify due_at recalculated. Resolve ticket → change priority → verify due_at unchanged (frozen).

test.name: IT-CONCURRENCY: Concurrent priority changes with optimistic locking retry
test.description: Two threads update same ticket priority concurrently → both succeed with retry → verify final SLA matches last write. Measure retry exhaustion rate.

test.name: LT-PERFORMANCE: 1000 incidents/min with SLA tracking
test.description: Create 2000 incidents over 2 minutes with concurrent threads → measure p95/p99 latency → verify <400ms p95 (no degradation from baseline). Verify all SLA records created correctly.

path.name: Flyway Migration Version Correction
path.description: CRITICAL: Story originally specified V8__create_sla_tracking_table.sql, but V8/V9/V10 already exist. Must create V11__create_sla_tracking_table.sql instead.
path.impact: HIGH - Migration will fail if wrong version number used

path.name: TicketService Modification Strategy
path.description: Story 2.3 MODIFIES existing TicketService from Story 2.2 (Approved). Changes must be additive (inject new dependencies, add logic in createTicket/updatePriority). Do not refactor existing methods.
path.impact: MEDIUM - Minimize risk of regressing Story 2.2 functionality

path.name: SLA Recalculation from Original createdAt
path.description: When priority changes, recalculate due_at from ORIGINAL ticket.createdAt (not current time). This preserves fairness - if ticket created 1h ago as LOW (24h SLA), then escalated to CRITICAL (2h SLA), it's already breached.
path.impact: MEDIUM - Correct business logic for SLA enforcement

implementation_order.step: Create Flyway migration V11 first, test with ./gradlew flywayMigrate
Create SlaTracking entity and SlaTrackingRepository, verify persistence with repository test
Create SlaCalculator service with unit tests (6 tests), achieve >90% coverage
Inject SlaCalculator + SlaTrackingRepository into TicketService constructor
Modify TicketService.createTicket() to add SLA tracking logic after ticket persistence
Modify TicketService.updatePriority() to recalculate SLA (with status checks)
Update existing TicketService unit tests to mock SLA components (avoid test failures)
Create load tests (SlaTrackingLoadTest), run and document results
Create ADR-011, update architecture docs, complete code review

gotcha.name: Service Request Exclusion
gotcha.description: Only INCIDENT tickets get SLA tracking. Check ticket.getTicketType() == TicketType.INCIDENT before creating SLA record. Service requests have approval workflows instead.

gotcha.name: Null Priority Handling
gotcha.description: If ticket created without priority, no SLA record is created. If priority added later via updatePriority(), create SLA record at that time (AC-6 "If not found, create new SLA record").

gotcha.name: Resolved/Closed Ticket SLA Freeze
gotcha.description: AC-7 requires checking ticket status before recalculating SLA. If status is RESOLVED or CLOSED, skip recalculation to preserve historical accuracy.

gotcha.name: Unique Constraint on ticket_id
gotcha.description: sla_tracking table has UNIQUE constraint on ticket_id (one SLA record per ticket). Attempting to insert duplicate will throw constraint violation - handle gracefully in tests.

reference.title: Epic 2 Tech Spec - Story 2.3 Definition
reference.relevance: Authoritative source for Story 2.3 scope and acceptance criteria

reference.title: Epic 2 Tech Spec - SLA Policy
reference.relevance: Defines SLA durations: Critical 2h, High 4h, Medium 8h, Low 24h

reference.title: Epic 2 Tech Spec - TicketService Integration Example
reference.relevance: Shows exact code pattern for SLA tracking in createTicket() method

reference.title: Solution Architecture - Data Architecture
reference.relevance: Defines UUIDv7 primary key standard and OLTP schema approach

reference.title: Module Boundaries - ITSM Internal Package Structure
reference.relevance: Defines package-private visibility rules for internal/ components

reference.title: Story 2.2-REVISED - TicketService Foundation
reference.relevance: Approved Story 2.2 provides TicketService to integrate SLA tracking into

reference.title: Story 2.1 - Ticket Domain Entities
reference.relevance: Complete Story 2.1 provides Ticket, Incident domain entities referenced by SlaTracking
